channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iran-Backed Iraqi Militias Begin Disarming Amid US Pressure
item.description: Powerful Iran-backed militias in Iraq say they will start handing in weapons to authorities, a significant shift as the US-Iran conflict tests its ceasefire. The move signals potential de-escalation in the region. Source: AP News

item.title: 🚀 Blue Origin Rocket Explodes During Launch, Key Components Survive
item.description: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin confirmed its New Glenn rocket exploded during launch, but fuel tanks and critical launch pad parts were spared. The failure marks a setback for the company's orbital ambitions. Source: AP News

item.title: 🔴 NASA Declares Mars Maven Spacecraft Dead After Six Months of Silence
item.description: NASA has officially declared its Mars Maven spacecraft dead after losing contact six months ago. The orbiter had been studying the Martian atmosphere since 2014. Source: AP News

item.title: 🛢️ Shell Continued Oil Extraction Despite Nigeria Pipeline Pollution Evidence
item.description: Documents reveal Shell pumped oil through a Nigerian pipeline for years despite evidence of pollution. The company says the documents ignore context of the complex operating environment. Source: BBC

item.title: 🔥 Delhi Hotel Fire Kills at Least 21, Many Foreign Nationals Among Victims
item.description: A fire at a hotel in New Delhi killed at least 21 people, including many foreign nationals who had travelled to India for medical treatment or to accompany relatives. Source: BBC

item.title: 🇳🇴 Norway joins French nuclear deterrence pact as trust in US security guarantees wavers
item.description: Norway became the ninth country to sign onto France's nuclear deterrence framework, marking a significant shift in European security thinking as doubts grow about American commitment to NATO. Source: Defense News

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky threatens to fire officials over delays in Patriot missile contract implementation
item.description: Ukraine's president set a one-week deadline for completing the legal groundwork for a Patriot air defense agreement, warning of "serious personnel decisions" if progress stalls. Source: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🚁 Ukraine releases footage of drones striking Russian warship at Baltic Fleet base near St. Petersburg
item.description: Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces published video of drones attacking a Russian warship armed with guided missiles at the Kronstadt naval base, demonstrating Kyiv's reach into Russia's Baltic waters. Source: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden prepares special prisons for 13-year-old gang killers amid Nordic crime surge
item.description: Swedish authorities are building specialized prison facilities for teenagers recruited by gangs to carry out murders and bombings, as organized crime networks increasingly target minors across Scandinavia. Source: SCMP

item.title: 🇰🇼 One killed, dozens wounded in Iranian attack on Kuwait International Airport
item.description: Iranian drone and missile strikes hit Kuwait's international airport, killing one person and wounding more than 60 others, as regional conflict escalates despite ceasefire negotiations. Source: Al Jazeera

item.title: 🇬🇭 Ghana passes sweeping law criminalizing LGBTQ+ activity amid public panic
item.description: Ghana has passed a sweeping law criminalizing LGBTQ+ activity, with people panicking over the new restrictions in the West African nation. Source: The Guardian

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iran-Backed Iraqi Militias Agree to Hand Over Weapons
item.description: Powerful Iran-backed militias in Iraq announced they will begin handing in weapons to authorities, a significant development in regional de-escalation efforts. Source: AP News

item.title: 💰 US Announces 10%+ Tariffs on Most Trading Partners Over Forced Labor
item.description: The Trump administration announced plans for additional tariffs of 10% or more on most US trading partners following a forced labor investigation. The move affects allies including the UK, EU, Canada, Australia and Taiwan. Source: AP News

item.title: 🇮🇹 Four Migrant Workers Burned Alive in Italy Car Attack
item.description: Four migrant workers were reportedly burned alive in their car in Calabria, Italy, in an attack that highlights the widespread exploitation of foreign farm laborers in southern Europe. Source: The Guardian

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Protests Over Murder Case Turn Violent, 11 Officers Wounded
item.description: Eleven police officers were wounded in Southampton as demonstrators pelted them with rocks and garbage cans during protests over a murder case. British leaders urged calm. Source: New York Times

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drones Strike St Petersburg Oil Terminal During Putin Economic Forum
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck oil facilities in St Petersburg, setting an oil terminal ablaze as the city hosts an annual economic forum that Putin is scheduled to address. The attack disrupts Russia's flagship business event. Source: BBC

item.title: 🇭🇺 Orban Ordered Seizure of Ukrainian Gold Shipment
item.description: Hungarian PM Viktor Orban personally ordered the seizure of a Ukrainian bank convoy in retaliation for the suspension of oil transit through the Druzhba pipeline, according to Hungarian media reports. Source: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Launches New Attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: Iran conducted fresh drone and missile attacks targeting Kuwait and Bahrain as US Central Command conducted self-defense strikes on an Iranian military ground control station on Qeshm Island. Source: The War Zone

item.title: 🇩🇰 Denmark Gets First Government With Majority Women Ministers
item.description: For the first time in Danish history, the new cabinet will consist of more women than men as the PM presents a new government coalition. Source: SCMP

item.title: ☢️ US May Consider Placing Nuclear Weapons in Poland and Baltic States
item.description: While discussions are ongoing, an agreement to expand US nuclear hosting was not imminent, according to an unnamed source cited by the Financial Times. Source: Defense News

item.title: 🇺🇦 Seven Killed as Drone Strikes Bus in Russian-Controlled Crimea
item.description: A bus traveling between Moscow and Simferopol in Russian-occupied Crimea was hit by a drone attack, killing seven people. The strike highlights the expanding scope of drone warfare in the Ukraine conflict. Source: BBC

item.title: 🇬🇧 British Couple Loses Iran Jail Sentence Appeal
item.description: Lindsay and Craig Foreman, arrested in January 2025 during a global motorbike journey, have lost their appeal against jail sentences in Iran. The case adds to tensions over detained foreign nationals. Source: BBC

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada Seeks 16-Year Renewal of North American Trade Pact
item.description: Canada has formally requested a 16-year renewal of the USMCA trade agreement with the US and Mexico. The move comes as the three nations seek to maintain economic stability amid global uncertainty. Source: BBC

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK-China Relations Thaw as Foreign Secretary Hails New Ties
item.description: British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper emphasized "candour and respect" in renewed relations with Beijing, signaling a thaw in UK-China diplomatic relations despite ongoing differences on key issues. Source: Al Jazeera

item.title: 🛢️ OECD Warns of Dark Scenario if Gulf Energy Crisis Persists
item.description: The OECD has warned that prolonged disruption in the Gulf could send global growth rates tumbling to levels rarely seen outside major events like the Covid-19 pandemic, as the Iran conflict threatens energy supplies. Source: Financial Times
